interactions.summary: Jiang says the greater war is spiritual rather than merely military and tells listeners they must speak up, become light in darkness, and prove their humanity through courage and kindness.
The interviewer paraphrases Jiang's ethic by saying real wealth is inner knowledge and doing right in this short life, and Jiang affirms him.
Jiang adds that the only available action is to be a good person and show kindness during tribulation, then turns to the practical question of whether he is back to regular lectures.
Jiang says he is back to giving three lectures a week and media interviews, but he is trying to pace himself because the war is accelerating and he wants to preserve his sanity and health.

speaker_notes.note: The packet boundary catches Jiang mid-transition from moral exhortation into logistics about his lecture schedule, and seg-0183 is effectively empty.

claims.claim: Jiang says the great war ahead is not fundamentally one of weapons but of spirit, consciousness, and whether people awaken to who they are.
Jiang says people cannot morally sit out this moment, because speaking up encourages others through the law of collective effect.
Jiang says proving one's humanity begins with the courage to be human even under conditions of darkness and powerlessness.
The interviewer says real wealth is inner knowledge and right action in a short human life, rather than external riches.
Jiang says the only available response in tribulation is to be a good person and show kindness.
Jiang says he is back to a schedule of roughly three lectures a week and is trying to manage his pace because the war is accelerating and public attention on him has exploded.

signature_moments.moment: The decisive conflict is described as a war of spirit rather than a war of weapons.
signature_moments.source_phrase: It's not one of weapons. It's really one of spirit
signature_moments.why_it_matters: Jiang compresses his whole moral and political frame into a single reversal of what counts as the real battlefield.

signature_moments.moment: One person speaking becomes the light that gives others permission to do the same.
signature_moments.why_it_matters: The packet transforms collective effect from theory into a charged image of moral contagion.

signature_moments.moment: Humanity has to be proven through courage.
signature_moments.why_it_matters: Jiang turns being human from a biological fact into an existential task under pressure.

glossary_terms.definition_or_usage: Jiang's claim that one person's courageous action encourages others because human beings are spiritually and socially interconnected.
Jiang's term for the deeper conflict over consciousness, courage, and moral awakening beneath physical war.
The interviewer's synthesis, affirmed by Jiang, that wealth is inner knowledge and right action rather than money or status.

chronology_notes.note: This packet turns Jiang's end-times diagnosis into direct moral exhortation and then into a practical update on how he plans to continue speaking through the acceleration.

uncertainty_notes.note: The seg-0182 transition is syntactically rough in ASR, but the moral instruction and the return-to-lectures update are both clear enough to preserve.
